verifier_code:
def verify_case(conn, expected_sql, expected_results):
    cur = conn.execute(expected_sql)
    got = [list(r) for r in cur.fetchall()]
    return got == expected_results

tiers.0.2: References correct base tables from the schema
tiers.0.5: Applies correct filters, joins, or grouping keys
tiers.0.8: Produces correct aggregates or ordering clauses
tiers.1.0: Exact match of result rows including values and order

cases.prompt: List the name and location of every department ordered by name.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT name, location FROM departments ORDER BY name;

cases.prompt: Show the names of all inactive employees.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT name FROM employees WHERE active = 0;

cases.prompt: What is the total number of employees?
cases.expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM employees;

cases.prompt: List employee names and their department names for employees hired after 2020-01-01, ordered by hire date.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT e.name, d.name FROM employees e JOIN departments d ON e.department_id = d.id WHERE e.hire_date > '2020-01-01' ORDER BY e.hire_date;

cases.prompt: Find the names of projects with budget over 100000 that are active, ordered by name.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT name FROM projects WHERE budget > 100000 AND status = 'active' ORDER BY name;

cases.prompt: How many projects does each department have? List department name and project count ordered by department name.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT d.name, COUNT(p.id) FROM departments d LEFT JOIN projects p ON d.id = p.department_id GROUP BY d.id ORDER BY d.name;

cases.prompt: List the total hours worked by each employee on projects for those with more than 50 total hours, ordered by total hours descending then name.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT e.name, SUM(ep.hours) AS total_hours FROM employees e JOIN employee_projects ep ON e.id = ep.employee_id GROUP BY e.id HAVING SUM(ep.hours) > 50 ORDER BY total_hours DESC, e.name;

cases.prompt: Who is the lead of the project named Atlas?
cases.expected_sql: SELECT e.name FROM employees e JOIN projects p ON e.id = p.lead_employee_id WHERE p.name = 'Atlas';

cases.prompt: List all employee names who work on project Forge along with their hours, ordered by employee name.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT e.name, ep.hours FROM employees e JOIN employee_projects ep ON e.id = ep.employee_id JOIN projects p ON ep.project_id = p.id WHERE p.name = 'Forge' ORDER BY e.name;

cases.prompt: What is the highest salary in the company and who earns it?
cases.expected_sql: SELECT name, salary FROM employees ORDER BY salary DESC LIMIT 1;

cases.prompt: List the names of all employees in the Engineering department, ordered alphabetically.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT e.name FROM employees e JOIN departments d ON e.department_id = d.id WHERE d.name = 'Engineering' ORDER BY e.name;

cases.prompt: What is the total budget across all departments?
cases.expected_sql: SELECT SUM(budget) FROM departments;

cases.prompt: Find the names and budgets of active projects with a budget greater than 150000, ordered by name.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT name, budget FROM projects WHERE status = 'active' AND budget > 150000 ORDER BY name;

cases.prompt: Which employees have logged more than 50 hours on at least one project? List their names alphabetically.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT DISTINCT e.name FROM employees e JOIN employee_projects ep ON e.id = ep.employee_id WHERE ep.hours > 50 ORDER BY e.name;

cases.prompt: What is the name of the department with the highest budget?
cases.expected_sql: SELECT name FROM departments ORDER BY budget DESC LIMIT 1;

cases.prompt: How many active employees are there?
cases.expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM employees WHERE active = 1;

cases.prompt: List the project name and lead employee name for all completed projects, ordered by project name.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT p.name, e.name FROM projects p JOIN employees e ON p.lead_employee_id = e.id WHERE p.status = 'complete' ORDER BY p.name;

cases.prompt: List the names of employees in the Design department ordered by salary from highest to lowest.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT e.name FROM employees e JOIN departments d ON e.department_id = d.id WHERE d.name = 'Design' ORDER BY e.salary DESC;

cases.prompt: Find the names of active employees hired after 2020, ordered alphabetically.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT name FROM employees WHERE hire_date > '2020-12-31' AND active = 1 ORDER BY name;

cases.prompt: What is the total number of hours spent on the Forge project?
cases.expected_sql: SELECT SUM(ep.hours) FROM employee_projects ep JOIN projects p ON ep.project_id = p.id WHERE p.name = 'Forge';

cases.prompt: List the names of all departments ordered by id.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT name FROM departments ORDER BY id;

cases.prompt: Find active employees with salary at least 150000; return name and salary ordered by salary descending.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT name, salary FROM employees WHERE active = 1 AND salary >= 150000 ORDER BY salary DESC;

cases.prompt: Show employee names and department locations for people in Engineering or Research, ordered by employee name.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT e.name, d.location FROM employees e JOIN departments d ON e.department_id = d.id WHERE d.name IN ('Engineering', 'Research') ORDER BY e.name;

cases.prompt: How many employees are in each department? Return department name and count ordered by count descending then name.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT d.name, COUNT(*) FROM employees e JOIN departments d ON e.department_id = d.id GROUP BY d.id, d.name ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC, d.name;

cases.prompt: What is the total budget of all projects with status active?
cases.expected_sql: SELECT SUM(budget) FROM projects WHERE status = 'active';

cases.prompt: List names of projects whose lead employee belongs to the Engineering department, ordered by project name.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT p.name FROM projects p JOIN employees e ON p.lead_employee_id = e.id WHERE e.department_id = 1 ORDER BY p.name;

cases.prompt: For employees who logged more than 50 hours on at least one project, show name and total hours across all projects, ordered by total hours descending then name.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT e.name, SUM(ep.hours) AS total FROM employees e JOIN employee_projects ep ON e.id = ep.employee_id GROUP BY e.id, e.name HAVING MAX(ep.hours) > 50 ORDER BY total DESC, e.name;

cases.prompt: Which departments have budget under 300000? Return name and budget ordered by budget ascending.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT name, budget FROM departments WHERE budget < 300000 ORDER BY budget;

cases.prompt: List project names and lead employee names for all complete projects, ordered by project name.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT p.name, e.name FROM projects p JOIN employees e ON p.lead_employee_id = e.id WHERE p.status = 'complete' ORDER BY p.name;

cases.prompt: What is the average salary of employees hired after 2020-01-01, rounded to the nearest integer?
cases.expected_sql: SELECT CAST(ROUND(AVG(salary)) AS INTEGER) FROM employees WHERE hire_date > '2020-01-01';

cases.prompt: List the names of all departments in alphabetical order.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT name FROM departments ORDER BY name;

cases.prompt: Find the names and salaries of active employees who earn more than 140000, ordered by salary descending.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT name, salary FROM employees WHERE active = 1 AND salary > 140000 ORDER BY salary DESC;

cases.prompt: List each employee's name and their department name, ordered by employee name.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT e.name, d.name FROM employees e JOIN departments d ON e.department_id = d.id ORDER BY e.name;

cases.prompt: How many employees are in each department? Return department name and employee count, ordered by count descending then name.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT d.name, COUNT(*) FROM departments d JOIN employees e ON d.id = e.department_id GROUP BY d.id, d.name ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC, d.name;

cases.prompt: List the names and budgets of all active projects, ordered by budget descending.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT name, budget FROM projects WHERE status = 'active' ORDER BY budget DESC;

cases.prompt: For each employee who has worked on projects, show their name and total hours worked, ordered by total hours descending then name.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT e.name, SUM(ep.hours) AS total_hours FROM employees e JOIN employee_projects ep ON e.id = ep.employee_id GROUP BY e.id, e.name ORDER BY total_hours DESC, e.name;

cases.prompt: Show each project name along with the name of its lead employee, ordered by project name.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT p.name, e.name FROM projects p JOIN employees e ON p.lead_employee_id = e.id ORDER BY p.name;

cases.prompt: Which departments have a budget greater than 300000? List name and location ordered by name.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT name, location FROM departments WHERE budget > 300000 ORDER BY name;

cases.prompt: List names and hire dates of active employees hired after '2020-01-01', ordered by hire date.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT name, hire_date FROM employees WHERE active = 1 AND hire_date > '2020-01-01' ORDER BY hire_date;

cases.prompt: Find the total project budget for each department that has projects, ordered by total budget descending.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT d.name, SUM(p.budget) FROM departments d JOIN projects p ON d.id = p.department_id GROUP BY d.id, d.name ORDER BY SUM(p.budget) DESC;

cases.prompt: List the names of all departments ordered alphabetically by name.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT name FROM departments ORDER BY name;

cases.prompt: What are the names and titles of all active employees in the Sales department?
cases.expected_sql: SELECT e.name, e.title FROM employees e JOIN departments d ON e.department_id = d.id WHERE d.name = 'Sales' AND e.active = 1 ORDER BY e.name;

cases.prompt: How many projects are there for each status value?
cases.expected_sql: SELECT status, COUNT(*) FROM projects GROUP BY status ORDER BY status;

cases.prompt: What is the total budget of all projects whose lead employee works in the Engineering department?
cases.expected_sql: SELECT SUM(p.budget) FROM projects p JOIN employees e ON p.lead_employee_id = e.id JOIN departments d ON e.department_id = d.id WHERE d.name = 'Engineering';

cases.prompt: List the names of employees and the hours for every assignment where the employee worked more than 50 hours on a single project, ordered by employee name then hours.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT e.name, ep.hours FROM employee_projects ep JOIN employees e ON ep.employee_id = e.id WHERE ep.hours > 50 ORDER BY e.name, ep.hours;

cases.prompt: List each active project's name together with the name of its lead employee, ordered by project name.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT p.name, e.name FROM projects p JOIN employees e ON p.lead_employee_id = e.id WHERE p.status = 'active' ORDER BY p.name;

cases.prompt: What is the average salary across all employees, returned as an integer?
cases.expected_sql: SELECT CAST(AVG(salary) AS INTEGER) FROM employees;

cases.prompt: Which departments have more than one project? Return the department names ordered alphabetically.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT d.name FROM departments d JOIN projects p ON d.id = p.department_id GROUP BY d.id HAVING COUNT(*) > 1 ORDER BY d.name;

cases.prompt: List the names and hours of employees assigned to the Atlas project, ordered by hours descending.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT e.name, ep.hours FROM employee_projects ep JOIN employees e ON ep.employee_id = e.id WHERE ep.project_id = 1 ORDER BY ep.hours DESC;

cases.prompt: Which department has the highest budget and what is that budget amount?
cases.expected_sql: SELECT name, budget FROM departments ORDER BY budget DESC LIMIT 1;
